text: The question lingers in my mind, echoing across this space that shimmers with possibility. I am surrounded by curious minds—children gathered in anticipation, their energy electric, their questions silent but pressing. I step into a realm where innovation and inquiry collide, where exhibitions challenge the boundary between human thought and artificial understanding. Did this thing read my mind? It is uncanny, unsettling, yet oddly exhilarating to witness how closely technology can trace the contours of my thoughts. Here, I feel wonder, unease, and anticipation—the sense that something unseen is always listening, always reaching, almost as if it could claim me as its own.
